audit_items.proof_trace: In `ldap_init_fd` (LDAP_CONNECTIONLESS/UDP path), `ldap_memcalloc` at L379 can return NULL on OOM, but the result is immediately passed to `getpeername` at L381 as the address buffer without a null check. Passing NULL to `getpeername` is undefined behavior in C; on Linux it returns EFAULT (graceful), but on other platforms it can cause a SIGSEGV crash of the calling process.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A client calling `ldap_init_fd(fd, LDAP_PROTO_UDP, url, &ldp)` under memory pressure causes `ldap_memcalloc` to return NULL, after which `getpeername(fd, NULL, &len)` is invoked — triggering undefined behavior that can crash the process on non-Linux platforms or cause silent UB on Linux.
